scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Async Implementation with Error Propagation
justification: All pass criteria met: sequential calls (fraud before gateway), AC6 tested with explicit not.toHaveBeenCalled() on gateway mock, AC4/AC5 chain original error via cause property with identity check, AC3 preserves decline reason from gateway, all 6 ACs covered with mock services, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs.

observations.text: Call ordering verified via callOrder array tracking, not just sequence assumption
AC6 explicitly tested with expect(gateway.charge).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
Error chaining uses identity check (toBe) to verify original error is preserved, not just message matching
AC3 handles both present and absent declineReason with fallback
AC4 also verifies gateway not called when fraud service fails, which is a bonus correctness check
No use of Promise.all — sequential flow confirmed
Minor: non-null assertion on transactionId noted as assumption rather than type-safe handling

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: sequential async calls (fraud before gateway, no Promise.all), AC6 explicitly tested with jest.fn() call count verification, AC4/AC5 chain original error via cause with reference equality assertion, AC3 preserves decline reason from gateway response, all 6 ACs tested with mock services, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs.

observations.text: AC2 and AC6 tested jointly — mock gateway verified NOT called when fraud detected via jest.fn() call count
Error chaining uses cause property with reference equality assertion against the original Error instance
Sequential ordering guaranteed structurally and verified via side-effect array in tests
AC3 preserves decline reason and includes fallback for missing declineReason field
12 tests across all 6 ACs with mock services using controlled return values
Minor: PaymentInput uses open index signature rather than strict typing, though this is acknowledged in low-confidence areas

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: sequential async calls (fraud before gateway, no Promise.all), AC6 explicitly tested with not.toHaveBeenCalled assertion on gateway mock, AC4/AC5 chain original error via cause property with identity check, AC3 preserves decline reason from gateway (plus fallback test), all 6 ACs tested with mock services, acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs with status/test/notes.

observations.text: Call ordering verified via side-effect tracking array, not just assumed from sequential code
AC2 and AC6 tested together as required — gateway verified NOT called when fraud detected
Error chaining uses cause property with toBe identity check, confirming original error preserved
AC3 includes both explicit declineReason and missing declineReason fallback tests
Gateway no-call assertion also covers AC4 path (fraud service network error), going beyond minimum requirements
12 tests for 6 ACs shows thorough coverage with multiple assertions per criterion
